Published Recipes

      

                
                  

                    ID
                    Name

                  227
                  Southland Cheese Rolls - Pringle
                  Adjusted from "Southland Cheese Rolls"
                  
  • 1 can reduced cream
  • 1 tsp mustard powder
  • 500 g tasty cheese grated
  • 32 g onion soup powder
  • 35 slices sandwich bread
  • 1 spread mayonnaise or softened butter

                  35
                  Link
                  
  1. STEP 1 Place evaporated milk in a pot with cream onion mustard powder and pepper.
  2. Heat until almost boiling.
  3. Remove from heat add cheese and soup powder and stir until cheese has melted and sauce has thickened.
  4. Allow to cool before using (it will thicken further as it cools).
  5. STEP 2 To cook rolls preheat oven to 180˚C and line 2 oven trays with baking paper.
  6. STEP 3 Spread one side of each slice of bread with mayonnaise or butter and remove crusts if desired (traditionally they aren’t removed).
  7. Bake until crisp and golden (15-20 minutes).
  8. Allow to cool for a few minutes before serving.

                  111
                

              
                

                  226
                  Southland Cheese Rolls
                  
                  
  • 370 ml evaporated milk
  • 1 cup cream
  • 1 small onion grated
  • 1 tsp mustard powder
  • 1/2 tsp fine white pepper
  • 500 g edam cheese grated
  • 32g packet of onion soup powder
  • 35 slices sandwich bread 
  • mayonnaise or softened butter to spread

                  35
                  Link
                  
  1. STEP 1 Place evaporated milk in a pot with cream onion mustard powder and pepper.
  2. Heat until almost boiling.
  3. Remove from heat add cheese and soup powder and stir until cheese has melted and sauce has thickened.
  4. Allow to cool before using (it will thicken further as it cools).
  5. STEP 2 To cook rolls preheat oven to 180˚C and line 2 oven trays with baking paper.
  6. STEP 3 Spread one side of each slice of bread with mayonnaise or butter and remove crusts if desired (traditionally they aren’t removed).
  7. Bake until crisp and golden (15-20 minutes).
  8. Allow to cool for a few minutes before serving.

                  109
                

              
                

                  225
                  Prawn and egg fried rice
                  
                  
  • 2
  • 1/2 tbsp sunflower oil
  • 3 Coles Australian Free Range Eggs lightly beaten
  • 2 small red chillies seeds removed chopped
  • 6 spring onions finely shredded
  • 2 garlic cloves finely chopped
  • 400 g peeled green prawns tails intact
  • 200 g white rice cooked to packet instructions
  • 1/3 cup (80ml) soy sauce
  • 1 tsp sesame oil
  • 2 tbsp sweet chilli sauce
  • 1 cup (120g) frozen peas

                  4
                  Link
                  
  1. Heat 2 teaspoons sunflower oil in a wok or large frypan over medium- high heat.
  2. Add the egg and cook stirring for 1-2 minutes until softly scrambled.
  3. Remove egg from the pan and set aside.
  4. Add remaining 2 tablespoons sunflower oil to the pan then cook chilli spring onion and garlic stirring for 1 minute or until fragrant.
  5. Add prawns and cook for 2-3 minutes until opaque.
  6. Return egg to the pan with rice soy sesame oil sweet chilli sauce and peas then cook stirring for 2-3 minutes to warm through.
  7. Garnish with coriander and serve with lime wedges if using.

                  108
                

              
                

                  224
                  Panna Cotta With Berry Coulis
                  
                  
  • For the Panna Cotta
  • Double cream (150ml/150g)
  • Milk (75ml/75g)
  • Caster sugar (35g)
  • 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract (3g)
  • 1 1/4 teaspoons powdered gelatine (4g)
  • 1 tablespoon cold water (5g)
  • For the Berry Coulis
  • Frozen mixed berries (e.g., raspberries, strawberries, blueberries) (100g)
  • 1 tablespoon caster sugar (15g)
  • 1/2 tablespoon lemon juice (3g)
  • For Serving
  • Frozen mixed berries, defrosted (50g)
  • Sprig of mint

                  2
                  Link
                  
  1. Prepare the Gelatine:
  2. Sprinkle the gelatine over 1 tablespoon of cold water in a small bowl. 
  3. Let it bloom (absorb the water) for 5-10 minutes.
  1. Heat the Cream Mixture:
  2. In a saucepan, combine the double cream, milk, vanilla, and caster sugar.
  3. Heat gently over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture is just below the boiling point (do not let it come to a full boil).
  1. Add the Vanilla and Gelatine:
  2. Remove the cream mixture from the heat.
  3. Add the bloomed gelatine to the cream mixture and stir until the gelatine is fully dissolved.
  4. Strain to remove lumps and bubbles.
  1. Pour and Set:
  2. Pour the mixture into 2 small serving glasses or ramekins.
  3. Allow the panna cotta to cool to room temperature, then cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight to set.
  1. Make the Berry Coulis:
  2. In a small saucepan, add the frozen berries, lemon juice, and sugar.
  3. Heat over medium-low heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the berries have softened and released their juices.
  4. Remove the berry mixture from the heat and save some for garnishing the panna cotta.
  5. Blend or pass through a sieve until smooth.
  6. Let the coulis cool to room temperature, then refrigerate until ready to serve.
  1. Assemble:
  2. Once the panna cotta has set, spoon the berry coulis over the top of each serving.
  3. Add a few berries on top and a sprig of mint for garnish.

                  106
                

              
                

                  223
                  Chilli Bean Quesadillas
                  
                  
  • 4 Serves 10 Serves 25 Serves 50 Serves
  • 400 g chilli beans
  • 100 g frozen or fresh corn kernels
  • 60 g edam cheese grated small tortillas or wraps
  • 1277kJ Total Fat
  • 9.1 g Saturated Fat
  • 3.3 g Total Carbohydrate
  • 40.1 g Sugars
  • 4.6 g Dietary Fibre
  • 7.3 g Sodium
  • 358mg

                  4
                  Link
                  
  1. Mix together the chilli beans and corn kernels Spread the chilli beans and corn evenly over half of the tortillas Sprinkle the grated cheese over the chilli beans Place the remaining tortillas on top of the chilli beans and cheese Heat a frying pan over a low to medium heat without any oil Place the filled tortillas in the frying pan and cook slowly flipping after approximately 3-4 minutes until both sides are a golden brown Cut in half and serve hot with a salad Share this page title=Facebook class=social-share> title=Whatsapp class=social-share> title=Twitter class=social-share> title=LinkedIn class=social-share> id=Copylink class=copy-link data-toggle=popover data-container=body data-placement=top title=Link copied!
  2. data-content=> Heart disease is New Zealand’s single biggest killer You can save lives by funding research Donate now Heart Foundation Wellbeing Your heart Professionals Educators Get involved About us About us Careers Our locations Our business Latest news

                  105
                

              
                

                  222
                  Spanish Omelet
                  
                  
  • 1 Potato (peeled and thinly sliced)
  • 2T thinly sliced spring onion
  • 2t Mixed herbs
  • ¼ Brown onion (chopped)
  • 100g Beef mince Optional
  • 1t of oil
  • 2 eggs
  • 2T of milk
  • 50g grated cheese (leave a bit for on top)
  • Salt and pepper to taste

                  2
                  Link
                  
  1. Break the eggs into a bowl and beat together with the milk, salt and pepper.
  2. Add herbs and finely chopped spring onion. Set aside.
  3. Heat the oil in a deep non-stick frying pan over medium heat, then add the chopped brown onions.
  4. Fry them until they’re golden brown and slightly softened.
  5. If you are using meat, add this to the pan now and fry for 5-7 minutes.
  6. Chop the potatoes and put them into the pan (stirring constantly).
  7. Add the egg mixture to the pan, then sprinkle the grated cheese on top.
  8. Lower the heat and cover the pan with a lid. Cook for about 12 minutes (until the egg has set), then cut it into wedges and serve while it’s hot!

                  104
                

              
                

                  221
                  Chilli Beans Eggs Recipe
                  
                  
  • 1 tablespoon oil
  • 1 onion diced
  • 1 x
  • 400 gram can chilli beans
  • 1 x
  • 400 gram can chopped tomatoes or 4 tomatoes chopped
  • 2 cups corn kernels
  • 1 capsicum deseeded and sliced
  • 4 eggs

  2. Add onion and cook until soft.
  3. Add chilli beans tomatoes corn and 4.
  4. Make four holes in the mixture and break an 5.
  5. Cover with a lid and cook over low heat until 6.
  6. Remove from heat.

2. Add chicken pieces and fry on one side
3. Turn them over and do the same for the
4. Once browned and loose from the pan,
5. Add lemon juice and water and cook until

                  218
                  Poached mini meatballs
                  
                  
  • 1 x beef mixture
  • 2 cups water
  • 1/2 beef stock cube
  • 1 1/4 red onion diced
  • 1/2 tomato diced
  • 1 cup salad greens
  • 2 tablespoons grated cheese
  • 2 wholemeal bread rolls

                  2
                  
                  
  1. Use your hands to shape the beef mixture into golf-ball-sized balls.
  2. Heat 1 litre of water in a large saucepan until it boils. Add stock cube, red onion and tomato.
  3. Reduce the heat to a simmer. Use tongs or a slotted spoon to carefully place each meatball into the water, then simmer for 10 minutes, or until the meatballs are no longer pink in the middle.
  4. Remove meatballs from the water and place in a bowl.
  5. Divide the salad greens between serving dishes and pour over the poaching liquid. Sprinkle with cheese and serve with a wholemeal roll.

                  100
                

              
                

                  217
                  Baked mini meatloaf
                  
                  
  • oil spray
  • 1 X beef mixture
  • 1 tablespoon tomato sauce
  • 2 slices bread
  • 2 tablespoons grated cheese
  • 1 tomato sliced into wedges
  • 1 1/2 cup finely sliced lettuce or baby spinach leaves 2
  • 1/4 thinly sliced telegraph cucumber
  • 1/4 red onion sliced (optional)

                  2
                  
                  
  1. Preheat oven to 190°C.
  2. Grease the muffin pan with oil spray, divide the beef mixture equally among the cups, and smooth the tops with the back of a spoon.
  3. Spread ½ teaspoon of tomato sauce on top of each one with the spoon before placing them into the oven to bake.
  4. Place on the middle rack of the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes or until brown and no longer pink in the middle. Remove mini meatloaves from the oven, let cool slightly, then remove from the muffin pan and place on a plate. Food transformations
  5. Meanwhile, place bread on a baking tray and sprinkle cheese onto each slice. Place on the bottom rack of the oven for 5 minutes or until the cheese has melted. Remove from oven.
  6. Place meatloaves on serving plates with tomato, lettuce or baby spinach, cucumber, onion (if using) and cheese on toast.

                  99
                

              
                

                  216
                  Grilled kofta
                  
                  
  • 1 x beef mixture
  • 1 teaspoon oil
  • 2 pita breads cut in half
  • 1/2 cup sliced lettuce
  • 1/2 tomato sliced
  • 1/4 cup thinly sliced telegraph cucumber
  • 2 tablespoons cottage cheese

                  2
                  
                  
  1. Soak wooden skewers in a shallow bowl of water for 10 minutes.
  2. Heat the grill of the oven to medium heat.
  3. Use clean hands to mould a heaped tablespoon of beef mixture around a wooden skewer into a kofta-shaped sausage.
  4. Place in a baking dish. Repeat with the remaining beef mixture and skewers.
  5. Brush the kofta with a little oil and grill, turning carefully once, for 5-8 minutes or until browned on all sides and no longer pink in the middle.
  6. Remove from oven. Place koftas on a paper towel-lined board using tongs.
  7. Meanwhile, warm pita breads in the oven, then remove. Fill each half with lettuce, tomato, cucumber and cottage cheese. Food transformations
  8. Serve koftas with pita bread.

                  98
                

              
                

                  215
                  Deconstructed burger - Beef mixture
                  
                  
  • 200 grams lean beef mince
  • 1 egg
  • 1 /2 cup dry breadcrumbs
  • 1 carrot grated
  • 1 /4 onion finely diced
  • 1 /4 cup chopped parsley
  • 2 teaspoons tomato sauce/paste/puree

                  2
                  
                  
  1. Place all ingredients into a bowl and mix together until combined, using clean hands or a wooden spoon.
  2. Use this mix to create either a traditional burger or a deconstructed burger, e.g. grilled kofta, baked mini meatloaf, or poached mini meatballs.

                  97
                

              
                

                  214
                  Cheese Sauce Recipe
                  
                  
  • 2 tablespoons oil
  • 3 tablespoons flour
  • 2 cups milk
  • 1 cup grated cheese pinch salt
  • pepper to taste

                  4
                  Link
                  
  1. Heat oil in saucepan over medium heat and add flour.
  2. Stir and cook for approximately one minute.
  3. Gradually add milk in small amounts mixing until 4.
  4. Continue until all milk has been added.
  5. Return to a low heat and bring to a simmer stirring 6.
  6. Stir through grated cheese.
  7. Remove from heat.
  8. Season with salt and pepper.

                  96
                

              
                

                  213
                  Sweet Sour Pork Recipe Card
                  
                  
  • 425 grams pineapple pieces in juice
  • 2 teaspoons soy sauce
  • 1
  • ½ tablespoons vinegar
  • 1
  • ½ tablespoons tomato sauce
  • 2 teaspoons brown sugar
  • 1 teaspoon fresh ginger grated (optional)
  • 1 tablespoon cornflour
  • 1 tablespoon vegetable oil
  • 500 grams lean pork sliced thinly
  • 1 onion sliced
  • ½ capsicum chopped
  • 2 carrots peeled and sliced
  • ½ broccoli cut into florets
  • 2 cups cabbage shredded

                  4
                  Link
                  
  1. Sweet and sour sauce 1.
  2. In a saucepan place canned pineapple with juice soy sauce vinegar tomato sauce brown sugar and ginger.
  3. Bring to the boil then reduce heat to a gentle simmer 3.
  4. Wet the cornflour with a little water in a small bowl.
  5. Slowly pour the cornflour mixture into the simmering sauce while stirring constantly until it begins to thicken.
  6. Stir fry 1.
  7. Heat oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium heat.
  8. Add the pork and stir fry until brown on all sides remove from the pan and set aside.
  9. Add the vegetables to the pan and cook until tender.
  10. Add the pork back to the pan with the sweet and sour sauce and heat through.
  11. Remove from heat spoon into bowls and serve.

                  211
                  Courgette Fritters Recipe
                  
                  
  • 1 egg
  • 1/4 cup milk
  • 1/3 cup wholemeal flour
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1 courgette grated and liquid squeezed out
  • 1 teaspoon oil pinch salt
  • pepper to taste

                  2
                  Link
                  
  1. Beat the eggs and milk in a bowl.
  2. Stir in the flour baking powder courgette salt and pepper.
  3. Heat the oil in the frying pan over medium heat.
  4. Place spoonfuls of mixture into the pan 3-4 at a time.
  5. Cook on the other side until golden brown.
  6. Remove and place on a plate.
  7. Cook remaining mixture.
  8. Serving suggestion • Serve with rocket or green salad.
  9. Variation • Use a grated carrot or corn kernels in place of courgette.

                  Sweet Sour Chicken Recipe
                  
                  
  • 200 grams canned pineapple pieces in juice drained with juice reserved
  • 1 tablespoon cornflour
  • 1 tablespoon soy sauce

                  178
                  Popcorn Chicken Recipe
                  
                  
  • 200 grams boneless skinless chicken thighs sliced into bite-sized pieces
  • 2 tablespoons milk
  • 1/2 teaspoon lemon juice
  • 2 eggs
  • 1/2 cup flour
  • 1/2 cups breadcrumbs
  • 1 teaspoon dried herbs
  • 1 teaspoon paprika
  • 1 teaspoon lemon zest

                  2
                  Link
                  
  1. Preheat oven to 225 ° C.
  2. Place eggs flour and breadcrumbs in three separate bowls and place in a row on the table or bench.
  3. Beat eggs.
  4. Add dried herbs paprika and lemon zest into breadcrumbs and mix well.
  5. Next place eggy chicken pieces into breadcrumbs press breadcrumbs all over.
  6. Place coated chicken pieces on a baking tray.
  7. Bake in preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until golden brown and chicken is no longer pink inside.
  8. Remove from oven.
  9. Use tongs to remove chicken pieces from dish and serve.
  10. Serving suggestions • Serve in a wrap with salad vegetables.
  11. • Serve with mashed potato and coleslaw.

                  173
                  Spinach And Cheese Puff Tarts
                  
                  
  • 120 g Spinach
  • 1/2 cup Basil leaves shredded
  • 3/4 cup Cheddar cheese grated
  • 50 g Feta crumbled
  • 1 Small egg
  • 12 Cherry tomatoes

                  12
                  Link
                  
  1. Preheat an oven to 200˚C (180°C fan forced).
  2. Place the spinach into a microwave safe bowl.
  3. Cook for 1 minute to wilt.
  4. Remove and when cool enough to handle chop into pieces.
  5. Add the 3/4 of the basil Parmesan feta lemon zest egg nutmeg and salt and pepper combining well.
  6. Cut 12 x 8cm circles from the pastry sheets and place onto a baking tray.
  7. Place tablespoons of mixture into the middle of the pastry circles leaving a 1cm border.
  8. Place a tomato into the centre of each.
  9. Bake for 20 minutes until puffed and golden.
  10. Serve warm with a sprinkle of basil leaves.
  11. Follow the instructions on the packet to cook.

                  28
                

              
                

                  171
                  Fish Cakes
                  
                  
  • 500 g potatoes peeled
  • 30 g butter (or Olivani Spread)
  • 500 g white fish fillets
  • 1 small onion finely chopped
  • 2 Tbsp chopped tarragon or parsley
  • salt and pepper
  • ½ cup Edmonds Standard Grade Flour
  • 1 egg
  • 1 Tbsp water
  • 1–1½ cups dried breadcrumbs
  • oil for shallow frying
  • lemon wedges

                  
                  Link
                  
  1. Poach the fish in lightly salted water or steam it.
  2. Cool slightly.
  3. Flake the fish and mix it gently into the potato with the onion herbs and salt and pepper.
  4. Taste and adjust the seasoning then form the mixture into 8 flat cakes.
  5. Combine flour with salt and pepper.
  6. Lightly coat the fishcakes with the seasoned flour.
  7. Beat the egg with the water and dip the fish cakes first in the egg mixture then in the breadcrumbs.
  8. Heat a little oil in a heavy-based frying pan and fry the fish cakes over a moderate heat for 2–3 minutes on each side or until golden.
  9. Drain on paper towels and serve with lemon wedges.

                  38
                

              
                

                  170
                  Savoury Cheese Muffins
                  
                  
  • 1 Tbsp chopped celery
  • 1 Tbsp chopped red or green capsicum
  • 3/4 cup grated Meadow Fresh tasty cheese
  • 2 cups Edmonds Standard Grade Flour
  • 4 tsp Edmonds Baking Powder
  • 1 Pinch of cayenne pepper
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1 egg beaten
  • 50 g butter melted (or Olivani Spread)
  • 11/2 cups of Meadow Fresh milk

                  
                  Link
                  
  1. Preheat oven to 200ºC.
  2. Grease a standard 12-hole muffin tin.
  3. Put celery pepper and cheese into a bowl.
  4. Sift flour baking powder cayenne and salt into the bowl.
  5. Stir to combine.
  6. Quickly stir in egg butter and milk just to moisten.
  7. Bake for 15 minutes or until golden.
  8. Place on a wire rack to cool.

                  39
                

              
                

                  169
                  Easter Lamb With Hot Cross Bun Stuffing
                  
                  
  • 1.8-2 kg Quality Mark butterflied boneless lamb legs
  • 2 Tbsp olive oil
  • 2 garlic cloves finely grated
  • 2 tsp chopped fresh rosemary
  • 1 tsp flaky sea salt freshly ground black pepper
  • 2 Hot cross buns lightly toasted
  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 1 small brown onion finely diced
  • 2 garlic cloves crushed
  • 1 Tbsp finely chopped fresh rosemary Zest of 1 lemon
  • 2 Tbsp flat-leaf parsley chopped
  • salt and pepper to taste

                  8
                  Link
                  
  1. Tear the hot cross buns into small pieces and place them in a bowl.
  2. Heat the olive oil or butter in a pan over medium heat.
  3. Add the onion and cook for 5 to 6 minutes until soft and sweet.
  4. Add the garlic and rosemary and cook for another 30 seconds.
  5. Tip the onion mixture over the hot cross buns add the parsley then season lightly with salt and pepper.
  6. Toss gently to combine.
  7. Set aside to cool slightly.
  8. Lay the lamb out flat on a board skin side down.
  9. If it is uneven in thickness gently open it out so it is as even as possible.
  10. In a small bowl mix the olive oil lemon zest garlic rosemary salt and pepper.
  11. Rub this mixture all over the lamb.
  12. Spoon the stuffing evenly over the lamb leaving a small border around the edges.
  13. Roll the lamb up tightly from the long edge enclosing the stuffing.
  14. Tie securely with a kitchen string at 4-5 cm intervals to keep it in shape.
  15. If you have time cover the lamb and rest it in the fridge for 1 hour to help the flavours settle.
  16. Preheat the oven to 190°C fan bake.
  17. Place the lamb on a rack set over a roasting dish.
  18. Roast for 1 hour to 1 hour 15 minutes for medium or until a thermometer reads around 60°C in the thickest part.
  19. Remove from the oven loosely cover with foil and rest for at least 15 minutes.
  20. Slice thickly and serve with roast kūmara seasonal greens and plenty of gravy or pan juices.

                  40

                  165
                  Pork Lap Lap Recipe
                  
                  
  • 1 cup grated kūmara
  • 1 cup grated pumpkin
  • 2 tablespoons coconut cream
  • 2 teaspoons curry powder
  • 200 grams pork mince
  • 1 spring onion finely chopped
  • 1 cup chopped silverbeet or spinach
  • 2 teaspoons soy sauce

                  2
                  Link
                  
  1. Heat oven to 200°C.
  2. In a large bowl mix all ingredients together.
  3. Divide the mixture between the baking paper sheets and fold over to make an enclosed parcel.
  4. Place parcels on a baking tray and bake for 30 minutes.
  5. Remove from oven.
  6. Using tongs place a parcel on each serving plate then serve.
  7. Tips: Serve the parcels still wrapped so that the steam only escapes when they are unwrapped at the table.
  8. Use banana or lap lap leaves in place of baking paper.

                  Coleslaw Recipe

                  53
                

              
                

                  162
                  Banana Pikelets Recipe
                  
                  
  • 1 large banana peeled mashed
  • 1 egg
  • 1 tablespoon oil Berry sauce
  • 1 cup fresh or frozen berries
  • 2 tablespoons water

                  2
                  Link
                  
  1. Place banana and egg into a bowl and mix together using a fork.
  2. Heat ½ tablespoon of oil in a non-stick frying pan over medium heat.
  3. Place 3 to 4 tablespoon amounts of mixture into the pan.
  4. Cook pikelets for 1 minute on one side or until bubbles appear on the surface.
  5. Turn using a spatula and cook the other side for 1 minute or until golden.
  6. Add rest of oil to the pan then repeat steps 3 to 5 until mixture is finished then turn off heat.
  7. Serve with berry sauce on top.
  8. Place berries and water into a saucepan.
  9. Heat slowly over low heat with the lid on until berries have thawed and are bubbling a little.
  10. Remove lid and continue to cook over a low heat until most of the liquid has evaporated.
  11. • Serve with 2 tablespoons of reduced-fat plain yoghurt.

                  52
                

              
                

                  161
                  Deconstructed Burger - Traditional Burger
                  
                  
  • 1 beef mixture
  • 2 teaspoons oil
  • 2 wholemeal burger buns
  • 2 tablespoons tomato sauce
  • 2 lettuce leaves
  • 1/4 telegraph cucumber thinly sliced
  • 1/2 tomato sliced
  • 2 tablespoons grated cheese

                  2
                  Link
                  
  1. Divide the meat mixture into two, form into balls and flatten to make patties. 
  2.  Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. 
  3.  Pan-fry the patties until brown on both sides and no longer pink in the middle. 
  4. Remove patties from the frying pan and place on a paper towel. 
  5. Place burger buns on a serving plate. 
  6. Top with tomato sauce, lettuce, patty, cucumber, tomato and cheese.

                  158
                  Weet Bix Breakfast Cookies

  • 1 teaspoon cinnamon
  • 3/4 cup margarine
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla essence

  • 1 egg lightly beaten

  1. Preheat oven to 180°C.

  4. Add the dry mix to the margarine mix and stir to combine.
  5. Using 1/4cup measurements roll the mixture into small balls and place them on a lined baking tray.
  6. Lightly press each ball then bake for 20 minutes or until cooked.
  7. Cool on the baking tray for 5 minutes then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

                  Butter Chicken

  • 50 g Cumin

  • 565 g Tomato paste

  • 1.3 kg Liquid vegetable stock
  • 9.5 kg Mixed vegetables

                  131

                  155
                  Ginger Beef Rice Bowl
                  
                  
  • 190 g Canola oil
  • 2.3 kg Onion (brown) Diced
  • 3.1 kg Carrots Grated
  • 3.1 kg Parsnip Grated
  • 100 g Garlic minced
  • 200 g Ginger minced
  • 6.4 kg Lean beef mince
  • 310 g Sesame oil
  • 610 g Teriyaki sauce
  • 180 g Soy sauce
  • 250 g Oyster sauce
  • 180 g Brown Sugar
  • 40 g Raw sesame seeds
  • 1.1 kg Baby spinach
  • 3.7 kg Rice

                  120
                  Link
                  
  1. Cook rice according to instructions.
  2. Sauté onions carrots and parsnips over medium heat until soft.
  3. Add garlic and ginger and sauté until fragrant (about 1 minute).
  4. Add mince and brown on medium to high heat.
  5. Once mince is browned add sesame oil teriyaki sauce soy sauce oyster sauce and brown sugar.
  6. Simmer for 15 minutes.
  7. Add spinach to the mince mixture cover and gently simmer for 5 minutes until wilted then stir through evenly.
  8. Serve immediately on a bed of rice with a sprinkling of sesame seeds to garnish.
  9. Tips from the cook Use a mild teriyaki sauce that is not too strong in flavour.
  10. I like to use courgettes in place of parsnips when they are in season and cheap.
  11. Add bok choy to the mince mixture about 10 minutes before service and cook until softened.
  12. For this dish to look its best cook it as close to service as possible and ensure it is left to simmer for no longer than 30 minutes.
  13. Cook’s kōrero This quick and simple meal is full of flavour and a top pick for our tamariki.
  14. We can include about 100g of cooked vegetables per serve for our year 4-8 tamariki with them still loving the meal.

                  59
                

              
                

                  153
                  Lasagne
                  
                  
  • 370 g Mushrooms (white) diced
  • 80 g Garlic crushed
  • 15 g Rosemary
  • 15 g Thyme
  • 220 g Butter
  • 4 g Nutmeg
  • 140 g Wholemeal flour

                  131
                  Link
                  
  1. Add mushrooms silverbeet and garlic and cook down for 3-4 minutes.
  2. Once cooked add tinned tomato rosemary and thyme.
  3. In a large soup pot heat a teaspoon of oil then add butter and season.
  4. Once butter is melted and lightly browned add nutmeg and flour.
  5. Cook flour for 2-3 minutes.
  6. Pour in milk and bring to a slight simmer.
  7. Reserving some cheese for the top of the lasagne add cheese and allow the sauce to thicken.
  8. Place in oven preheated to 180 degrees and cook until pasta sheets are soft.
  9. Tips from the cook Serve with a side of garlic bread to meet grain requirements (at least 10g 0-3 20g 4-8 40g 9+).

                  58
                

              
                

                  152
                  Easy Beef Stroganoff
                  
                  
  • 600 g Quality Mark beef steaks sliced into strips
  • 30 g butter
  • 1 onion chopped
  • 250 g button mushrooms sliced
  • 3/4 cup beef stock
  • 2 Tbsp tomato paste
  • 250 ml sour cream
  • 1 tsp cornflour
  • pasta

                  4
                  Link
                  
  1. Preheat a non-stick pan to a moderate heat.
  2. Cook the beef in 3 batches.
  3. Cook one side until the first sign of moisture appears on the uncooked side then turn and cook the other side.
  4. Remove each batch and set aside.
  5. Melt butter in the pan add the onion and cook until soft.
  6. Add the mushrooms cook until tender.
  7. Add beef stock and tomato paste stir to combine and bring to the boil.
  8. Reduce heat and simmer for 2 minutes.
  9. Stir in the combined sour cream and cornflour.
  10. Stir well simmer for 1 minute.
  11. Return beef to pan and simmer for 2 minutes.
  12. Serve with noodles or pasta and either salad or seasonal vegetables.
  13. Nutrition Information per Serving (378g) This nutrition analysis is based on 4 serves and doesn't include the pasta / salad / vegetables to serve.

                  42
                

              
                

                  140
                  Russian Fudge
                  
                  
  • 3½ cups Chelsea White Sugar
  • 200 g sweetened condensed milk
  • 125 g butter
  • ½ cup Meadow Fresh Original Milk
  • 3 Tbsp Chelsea Golden Syrup (tin)
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 tsp vanilla essence

                  36
                  Link
                  
  1. Grease a 20cm cake tin.
  2. Cut two sheets of baking paper the width of the tin criss-cross the paper to line the tin making sure the sheets are long enough to extend over the sides (you can use the paper to lift the fudge out of the tin once it has set).
  3. Warm over a gentle heat until the Chelsea White Sugar has dissolved about 10-15 minutes.
  4. Bring to a gentle boil and cook for about 10-15 minutes until it reaches the soft ball stage (120°C).
  5. Remove from the heat and add the vanilla.
  6. Cool for 5 minutes then beat using an electric mixer until the fudge is creamy and thick and has lost its gloss (approximately 10 minutes).
  7. Pour into prepared tin and leave to cool then refrigerate until set.
  8. Once set use the paper to lift the fudge out of the tin and place onto a chopping board then cut into small squares.

                  33
                

              
                

                  139
                  Asparagus And Tuna Caesar Salad
                  
                  
  • Clove garlic crushed - 1
  • Anchovies chopped finely - 2
  • 1/2 cup Cream
  • 2 Tbsp Lemon juice
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • 1 Tbsp Olive oil
  • Asparagus trimmed - 1 bunch
  • Baby cos lettuce cut into wedges - 2
  • 450 g Tinned tuna in spring water drained
  • 1/2 cup Shaved parmesan
  • Eggs - 4
  • 1 cup Croutons

                  4
                  Link
                  
  1. In a small pan place the garlic and anchovies and cook for 3-4 minutes.
  2. Add the cream and bring to a simmer for a further 2 minutes until thickened.
  3. Remove from the heat and add the lemon juice.
  4. Season with salt and pepper and set aside to cool.
  5. Rub the asparagus with olive oil and fry in a pan at medium heat for 5-6 minutes or until slightly charred.
  6. Remove from heat and set aside to cool.
  7. Place wedges of lettuce in a bowl and toss through the dressing coating evenly.
  8. Add the drained tuna and shaved parmesan gently combining and season to taste.
  9. To poach the eggs half fill a frying pan with water bringing to a simmer.
  10. Break in the eggs and poach to your liking.
  11. Place the salad onto four plates.
  12. Top each with a poached egg asparagus and some croutons and enjoy!

                  30
                

              
                

                  138
                  Pinwheel Scones
                  
                  
  • 2 cups Edmonds Standard Grade Flour
  • 4 teaspoons Edmonds Baking Powder
  • 1 teaspoon salt
  • 2 Tablespoons Chelsea White Sugar
  • 60 g butter (or Olivani Spread)
  • ¾ - 1 cup of Meadow Fresh Original Milk
  • FILLING
  • 70 g butter
  • melted (or Olivani Spread)
  • ⅓ cup Chelsea Soft Brown Sugar - Packed
  • 1 Tablespoon Cinnamon
  • GLAZE (OPTIONAL)
  • 1 cup Chelsea Icing Sugar
  • ½ teaspoon vanilla
  • 1-3 Tablespoons water

                  25
                  Link
                  
  1. Dough Preheat Oven to 200C.
  2. Line a Roasting Dish or Baking Tray with cooking spray and baking paper and set aside.
  3. Sift Edmonds Standard Grade Flour Edmonds Baking Powder salt and Chelsea White Sugar into a medium bowl.
  4. Add cold Tararua Butter - cut into smallish pieces.
  5. Add almost all the Meadow Fresh Original Milk at once keeping a little aside in case.
  6. It should be soft and sticky if a little dry looking add the remaining Meadow Fresh Original Milk stir in quickly but be careful not to overstir (some say use a knife to cut in the milk).
  7. Knead and fold 8 - 10 times sprinkling flour as required.
  8. Then roll out to a square about 30 x 30cm (12in) 2 Filling Melt Tararua Butter in a small bowl.
  9. Add to this the Chelsea Soft Brown Sugar and cinnamon and mix well.
  10. Using a pastry brush or knife spread the butter & sugar mixture over the dough leaving a small edge all the way around (alternatively brush the Tararua Butter and add the combined sugar & cinnamon to it dry).
  11. Using a sharp knife slice the pinwheels between 1-2 cm thick and place in the prepared dish.
  12. Bake for 10-15 minutes until golden brown and remove from the oven.
  13. Glaze In a medium bowl add the sifted Chelsea Icing Sugar and vanilla.
  14. Add water a small amount at a time stirring well until you have a smooth but drizzle-able consistency.
  15. Serve still slightly warm.

                  35
                

              
                

                  137
                  Frittata
                  
                  
  • 50 g butter (or Olivani Spread)
  • 2 onions chopped
  • carrots
  • potatoes
  • 4 eggs
  • 4 Tbsp Meadow Fresh cream or milk
  • ¼ cup Puhoi valley grated parmesan cheese

                  
                  Link
                  
  1. Preheat the oven to 180˚C.
  2. Melt the butter in a large heavy-based ovenproof frying pan add the onions and cook gently until translucent about 10 minutes.
  3. Add the grated vegetables and meat if adding season well with salt and pepper and cook stirring for 10 minutes or until just tender.
  4. Preheat the grill.
  5. Beat together the eggs and cream or milk and pour over the vegetable mixture.
  6. Cook gently until the egg begins to cook at the edges then sprinkle over the parmesan and grill for 8–10 minutes until the egg is set.
  7. Serve cut into wedges.
  8. Add your review Your name Rating 5 Star 4 Star 3 Star 2 Star 1 Star Comments David Easy and delicious Jane Willingale Yum Carollyn Delish....

                  37
                

              
                

                  130
                  Chocolate Crackles Less Sugar
                  
                  
  • 4 cups Kellogg’s Rice Bubbles
  • ⅔ cup icing sugar mixture sifted
  • ⅓ cup cocoa powder
  • 1 cup desiccated coconut
  • 250 g block Copha

                  20
                  Link
                  
  1. Combine Rice Bubbles icing sugar cocoa and coconut in a large bowl.
  2. Place Copha in a small saucepan and stir over low heat remove from heat immediately once melted.
  3. Combine with Rice Bubble mixture.
  4. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour or until set.

                  57
                

              
                

                  113
                  Cheese Onion Scones
                  
                  
  • 1 ½ cups Edmonds Self Raising Flour
  • ¼ Teaspoon Salt
  • 25 g butter (or Olivani Spread)
  • 1 cup grated Cheese
  • 1 small onion very finely chopped
  • ¼ cup Meadow Fresh Original Milk approximately
  • 4 cups Edmonds Self Raising Flour (600g)
  • ¼ cup Chelsea White Sugar (56g)
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 300 ml cream (1 small bottle of cream)
  • 300 ml lemonade
  • 2 cups Edmonds Self Raising Flour (300g)
  • 1 tsp Edmonds Baking Powder

                  10
                  Link
                  
  1. 1 Sift Edmonds Self Raising Flour and salt into a bowl.
  2. Rub in Tararua Butter add cheese and onion.
  3. 2 Pour nearly all of the Meadow Fresh Original Milk in at once.
  4. Mix to a soft dough.
  5. Add more milk if the dough is dry.
  6. Place on a floured surface and knead lightly.
  7. 4 Place on a greased and floured oven tray bake in a hot oven (220deg C) for 15 minutes or until golden brown.

                  34
                

              
                

                  111
                  Super Moist Chocolate Cupcakes
                  
                  
  • ¾ cup + 2 Tbsp Edmonds Standard Grade Flour (131g)
  • ¼ cup + 2 Tbsp cocoa powder (38g)
  • ¾ tsp Edmonds Baking Soda
  • ¾ tsp Edmonds Baking Powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ cup Chelsea Soft Brown Sugar (100g)
  • ½ cup Chelsea White Sugar (113g)
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup Meadow Fresh Milk (125ml)
  • ¼ cup Olivani Pure Olive Oil (60ml)
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup boiling water (125ml)
  • 2 ½ cups Chelsea Icing Sugar (375g) (see tip below)
  • 2-3 Tbsp boiling water
  • 50 g butter
  • softened (or Olivani Spread)

                  
                  Link
                  
  1. 1 Preheat oven to 180°C conventional or 160ºC fan-forced.
  2. Line a 12-hole muffin tray with paper cases.
  3. 2 Sift the dry ingredients into a large bowl then add the sugars and stir in.
  4. Add the egg Meadow Fresh Milk Olivani Pure Olive Oil and vanilla then beat with a mixer until combined.
  5. Scrape the sides of the bowl then add the boiling water and mix until just combined (the batter will be runny).
  6. 3 Pour the batter into a measuring cup or jug (it’s easier and cleaner than using a spoon) and pour evenly into each paper case until ¾ filled (don’t over fill or the mixture will overflow).
  7. 4 Place in the oven and bake for 20-22 minutes or until a tester inserted in the centre comes out clean.
  8. 5 Berry Icing Mix icing sugar with hot water.
  9. Add Tararua Butter and beat until light and fluffy.

                  32
                

              
                

                  110
                  Banana Muffins
                  
                  
  • 1 ½ cups Edmonds Standard Grade Flour (225g)
  • 1 tsp Edmonds Baking Powder
  • 1 tsp Edmonds Baking Soda
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 3 large ripe bananas (mashed)
  • ¾ cup Chelsea White Sugar (170g)
  • 1 egg
  • 80 g butter
  • melted (or Olivani Spread)

                  
                  Link
                  
  1. 1 Preheat oven to 175°C bake.
  2. Line a 12 hole muffin tin with paper cases.
  3. 2 Sift together the Edmonds Standard Grade Flour Edmonds Baking Powder Edmonds Baking Soda and salt then set aside.
  4. 3 In a large bowl combine the bananas Chelsea White Sugar egg and melted butter.
  5. Fold in the flour mixture until only just combined.
  6. Bake for 20-25 minutes until muffins are cooked through and spring back when gently pressed.
  7. Additional tips Dust with Chelsea Icing Sugar to serve!
  8. - Make sure you use overripe bananas (these give a better flavour too) - Avoid over-mixing the batter - fold the dry ingredients in until only just combined.
  9. - Don’t over bake the banana muffns - a skewer inserted into the centre should come out with a few moist crumbs and the muffins should spring back when lightly pressed on top.
  10. You can also add chopped nuts or berries.

                  31
                

              
                

                  103
                  Cinnamon Cream Oysters Recipe | Chelsea Sugar
                  
                  
  • 2 eggs
  • ¼ cup Chelsea Caster Sugar
  • 2 tsp Chelsea Golden Syrup
  • 60 g Edmonds standard flour
  • 1 tsp Edmonds baking powder
  • ½ tsp Edmonds baking soda
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • ½ tsp ground ginger
  • FILLING
  • whipped cream Chelsea Icing Sugar

                  12
                  Link
                  
  1. Preheat the oven to 200ËšC.
  2. Lightly butter twelve small shallow patty tins and dust them with flour.
  3. Warm a mixing bowl by running hot water inside it and then dry it thoroughly.
  4. Beat the eggs and Chelsea Caster Sugar until pale and thick.
  5. This will take about 3 minutes with an electric beater.
  6. Add the Chelsea Golden Syrup and keep beating for another 5 minutes.
  7. Spoon the mixture into the prepared tins and bake for 8-10 minutes.
  8. The surface should spring back when you touch it lightly with your finger.
  9. Leave for a couple of minutes until the mixture shrinks away from the tins a little.
  10. Turn out carefully loosening them with a round-bladed knife if necessary.
  11. When cold cut a slit into the side of each cake with a small serrated knife and spoon or pipe in some whipped cream.
  12. Dust with Chelsea Icing Sugar.
  13. Recipe courtesy of Edmonds

                  8
                

              
                

                  102
                  HFG crispy sweet chilli chicken - Healthy Food Guide
                  
                  
  • ¼ cup cornflour
  • 1 teaspoon each paprika onion powder garlic powder
  • 500 g skinless boneless chicken thighs cut into bite-sized chunks
  • spray oil
  • 1 teaspoon canola oil
  • Slaw
  • ¼ red cabbage finely shredded
  • ½ small beetroot grated
  • 1 cup fresh mint leaves
  • 1 cup fresh coriander leaves
  • 3 spring onions thinly sliced ¹⁄³ reserved for garnish
  • 1 green chilli sliced
  • 1 lime juice
  • 1 teaspoon fish sauce
  • Sweet chilli sauce
  • 2 teaspoons sesame oil
  • 1 tablespoon reduced-salt soy sauce
  • 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar
  • 2 tablespoons sweet chilli sauce
  • 2 teaspoons hoisin sauce
  • 1 teaspoon runny honey
  • 1 teaspoon dried chilli flakes
  • 2 fresh red chillies sliced to garnish (optional)
  • sesame seeds toasted to garnish

                  4
                  Link
                  
  1. 1 Preheat oven to 210°C/190°C fan.
  2. Line a large oven tray with baking paper.
  3. 2 In a large bowl combine cornflour, paprika, onion and garlic powders.
  4. Add chicken and toss to coat.
  5. Transfer to lined tray, spray with oil and bake for 25-35 minutes or until golden and slightly crispy.
  6. 3 Meanwhile, in a bowl, combine cabbage, beetroot, mint, coriander, spring onions and chilli, if using.
  7. Add lime juice and fish sauce and toss to combine.
  8. 4 In a small bowl, whisk sauce ingredients to combine.
  9. 5 In a non-stick fry pan, heat canola oil over medium high heat.
  10. Pour in sauce and bring to a simmer.
  11. Add the cooked chicken and stir to evenly coat.
  12. Remove from heat before sauce thickens too much and chicken loses its crispiness.
  13. 6 Serve chicken with rice, beans and slaw, garnished with sliced chilli, if using, sesame seeds and the remaining spring onions.

                  16
                

              
                

                  101
                  Olivani Fudgy Brownie
                  
                  125 g Olivani Original Spread
2/3 cup Chelsea Caster Sugar
1/2 cup Chelsea Brown Sugar
100 ml Meadow Fresh Standard Milk
Dark chocolate
chopped
250 g divided
1 tsp Vanilla extract
1 tsp Instant coffee
1 tsp Sea salt flakes (optional)
1 1/13 cups Edmonds standard plain flour
1/2 cup Cocoa powder
3/4 tsp Salt
1/2 tsp Edmond baking powder		
                  24
                  Link
                  1. Preheat oven to 180ºC bake. Grease an 18cm x 28cm slice tin and line with baking paper.
2. Place Olivani
sugars and milk in a large saucepan. Stir over medium-low heat until Olivani has melted and sugar has mostly dissolved.
3. Remove from heat and add 150g chopped chocolate and the vanilla. Stir until chocolate has melted.
4. Sift in flour
cocoa powder
coffee
salt and baking powder. Stir until combined. Gently stir through remaining 100g chopped chocolate.
5. Transfer to prepared tin. Bake for 20-25 minutes
until a skewer inserted into the centre comes out with a few moist crumbs. Sprinkle with sea salt flakes
if desired.
6. Place the tin on a wire cooling rack and allow to cool completely before slicing and serving.
                  27
                

              
                

                  100
                  Cubeez Inspired Fruit Salad
                  
                  400 g Honeydew
400 g Rockmelon
400 g Watermelon		
                  4
                  Link
                  1. Using a small sharp knife and a ruler as a guide
cut each piece of fruit into 18 x 2cm cubes.
2. Using image as a guide
stack fruit
alternating colours
to create a large block. Serve.
                  26
                

              
                

                  99
                  Potato And Bacon Bake
                  
                  Eggs
beaten - 4
350 ml Milk
Garlic cloves
finely chopped - 6
1 tsp Salt
Strips of bacon
finely diced - 4
Chives
3 Tbsp chopped
1 Tbsp Olive oil
Washed white potatoes
2 kg peeled and sliced thinly
Cheese
1 cup grated		
                  
                  Link
                  1. Preheat the oven to 190°C (170°C fan-forced)
2. In a large bowl
mix the eggs
milk
garlic
salt
bacon
and chives. Set aside.
3. Oil a 20cm square ovenproof dish
and layer one third of the potatoes evenly into the dish.
4. Pour one third of the egg mixture over the potatoes
making sure bacon and chives are evenly distributed. Repeat with the remaining two-thirds of the potatoes and egg mixture.
5. Sprinkle the cheese evenly on top. Cover loosely with foil and place in the oven for 45 minutes.
6. Uncover and bake a further 15 minutes until the potatoes are tender and everything is bubbling and golden.
                  25
                

              
                

                  98
                  Easy sweet and sour beef Recipe | Beef + Lamb New Zealand
                  
                  600 g Quality Mark beef rump steak
1 Tbsp dark soy sauce
½ tsp black pepper
300 g pumpkin
seeds removed
peeled and chopped
1 small head broccoli
chopped into florets
2 tsp olive oil
4 shallots
finely chopped
1 red capsicum
sliced
2 cups sweet and sour stir-fry sauce
white rice
Or swap out		
                  4
                  Link
                  Cut the beef across the grain into thin strips. Mix with 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce and a seasoning of pepper. Cover and set aside for 5-10 minutes. Add a little oil and mix well. Steam or microwave the pumpkin pieces and broccoli until just tender. Heat the wok ensure it is hot. Stir-fry the beef strips in 3 batches reserving each cooked batch to rest in a warm bowl. Reheat wok between each batch. Reheat the wok add oil and heat. Add the shallots and capsicum and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes. Add the cooked pumpkin broccoli and sweet and sour sauce stir-fry until sauce is hot. Return the beef to the wok toss to combine. Serve with noodles or rice.
                  9
                

              
                

                  97
                  Spaghetti And Cheese Toastie
                  
                  Bread
4 pieces sliced thickly
2 tsp Butter
1/2 cup Tinned spaghetti
Colby cheese
1/2 cup grated		
                  2
                  Link
                  1. Preheat a sandwich maker.
2. Butter one side of each slice of bread. Place the butter side down on the hot plate.
3. Top with 1⁄4 cup of spaghetti and 1⁄4 cup of Colby cheese
then add the second slice of bread butter side up.
4. Cook for 4 minutes. Let them sit for a couple of minutes before slicing them in half.
                  17
                

              
                

                  94
                  Cottage pie for kids Recipe | Beef + Lamb New Zealand
                  
                  750g Quality Mark beef mince
2 tsp olive oil
1 onion
finely chopped
1 carrots
finely chopped
1 celery stalk
finely chopped
1/4 cup tomato sauce
A pinch of black pepper
2 cups mixed vegetables
1 Tbsp butter
melted
1/2 cup water
1kg potatoes
peeled and chopped
1/2 cup milk
2 cups mixed vegetables		
                  6
                  Link
                  Preheat oven to 180ºC (160ºC fan). Heat the oil over a medium-high heat. Add onion carrot and celery. Cook for 5 to 7 minutes or until tender stirring often. Add mince and cook for a further 5 minutes or until mince is browned. Stir regularly to break up the mince. Add wine and cook for a further 4 minutes or until the liquid evaporates. Reduce heat to medium-low. Add tomato and Worcestershire sauces and season with pepper to taste. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and add frozen mixed vegetables and stir. Place potatoes in a saucepan. Cover with cold water and bring to the boil over a high heat. Reduce heat to medium. Cook uncovered for 15 to 20 minutes or until tender. Shake pan over low heat for 2 minutes to dry potatoes. Add milk and mash until smooth. Spoon mixture into a 6cm-deep (10 cup capacity) baking dish. Top with potato mash and brush with melted butter. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until piping hot and the potato mash is golden. As the cottage pie is finishing cook the frozen vegetables. Serve.
                  5
                

              
                

                  93
                  Lolly Cake Recipe | Chelsea Sugar
                  
                  24 Malt biscuits
¼ cup cherries (optional)
¼ cup coconut
16 marshmallows (best to use Explorer lollies)
½ - ¾
420g tin of condensed milk
Chelsea Icing Sugar for dusting		
                  12
                  Link
                  Crush biscuits very finely. Add a little of the coconut. Chop up the marshmallows and add the cherries (optional) Add the half tin condensed milk and mix well. You can either roll into balls or you can roll in to a log and then roll in the rest of the coconut then place in the fridge when hard cut into slices. Dust with Chelsea Icing Sugar.
                  3
                

              
                

                  91
                  Pikelets Recipe NZ | Edmonds Cooking
                  
                  1 cup Edmonds standard flour
1 tsp Edmonds baking powder
1/
4 tsp salt
1 egg
1/
4 cup Chelsea white sugar
3/
4 cup Meadow Fresh milk		
                  7
                  Link
                  Sift the flour and salt into a bowl. In another bowl beat the egg and sugar with a whisk until pale and thick. Add the egg mixture and the milk to the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Gently heat a non-stick frying pan and drop tablespoonfuls of the mixture from the point of the spoon onto the surface. When bubbles start to burst on the top of the pikelets turn them over and cook the second side until golden. Place in a clean tea towel to cool.
                  4
                

              
                

                  88
                  Macaroni Cheese Recipe Recipe NZ | Edmonds Cooking
                  
                  2 Tbsp butter (or Olivani Spread)
1 onion
finely chopped
2 Tbsp Edmonds standard grade flour
½ tsp dry mustard
2 cups Meadow Fresh milk
heated salt and pepper
2 cups Meadow Fresh grated tasty cheese
2 cups macaroni elbows
cooked
2 Tbsp dry breadcrumbs
Melt the butter in a saucepan
stirring
still stirring		
                  
                  Link
                  Preheat the oven to 190˚C.
Melt the butter in a saucepan
add the onion and cook gently for about 10 minutes or until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the flour and cook
stirring
until frothy. Then add the mustard.
Remove from the heat and add the hot milk gradually
stirring.
Return to the heat and cook
still stirring
until the sauce boils and thickens. Remove from the heat
season with salt and pepper and add half the grated cheese
and the macaroni.
Combine well and put into an ovenproof dish.
Mix the breadcrumbs into the remaining grated cheese and sprinkle over the top.
Cook for 20 minutes or until golden and heated through.
                  24
                

              
                

                  86
                  Boil Up Recipe (He Te Kōhua) Edmonds Cooking NZ
                  
                  1 tbsp salt
2kg meaty pork or bacon bones
2 medium potatoes
peeled and cut into chunks
2 medium kumara
peeled and cut into chunks
½ kamokamo or 2 medium carrots
½ small cabbage or 750g watercress
chopped
1½ cups Edmonds Self-Raising Flour + extra for rolling
1 cup Edmonds High Grade Flour
1 tsp salt		
                  
                  Link
                  Bring 2 litres of water to the boil in a large pot. Add the salt (no salt if using bacon bones).
Add the bones
cover and simmer for 1½ hours. Top up with water if it gets too low.
For the doughboys
mix the flours and salt with 1 cup warm water to form a soft dough. Divide and mould into 8 balls. Cover until needed.
Add the root vegetables to the pot and continue cooking gently for 5 minutes.
Add the greens and doughboys to the pot.
Boil gently with the lid on for 8–10 minutes
until the doughboys are big and fluffy. Serve and enjoy!
                  22
                

              
                

                  84
                  Beetroot Chutney Recipe NZ | Edmonds Cooking
                  
                  1kg beetroot
500g onions
2½ cups malt vinegar
1 Tbsp salt
2 cups Chelsea White Sugar
1 tsp ground allspice
1 tsp white pepper
1 tsp ground ginger
½ cup Edmonds Standard Grade Flour
½ cup malt vinegar		
                  3
                  Link
                  Steam or boil the unpeeled beetroot for 30 minutes or until tender.
When the beetroot have cooled a little
rub off the skins and finely chop the beetroot and onions – you can do this in a food processor.
Put the vegetables in a preserving pan with the first measure of vinegar and bring to a simmer. Cook until the onion is tender.
Add the salt
sugar
allspice
pepper and ginger and boil for 25 minutes
stirring occasionally.
Mix the flour to a smooth paste with the second measure of vinegar
add to the pan and bring to the boil
stirring. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Pack into sterilised jars and cover tightly. Keep for at least 2 weeks before using.
                  7
                

              
                

                  81
                  Ginger pork stir-fry - Healthy Food Guide
                  
                  1 lean pork steak
2 teaspoons minced ginger
3 tablespoons orange juice
1 tablespoon salt-reduced soy sauce
70g spaghetti
1 tablespoon olive oil
½ x 400g packet stir-fry vegetables (we used Wattie's Wok Creations Vietnamese Vegetables)
                  1
                  Link
                  1 Cut pork in thin strips and toss in ginger, orange juice and soy sauce. Marinate in a non-metallic dish for a few hours, or overnight.2 Cook spaghetti following packet directions until al dente. Drain. Set aside.3 Heat oil for a few minutes in a non-stick pan. Add pork and sear meat by cooking quickly. It should go golden at the edges.4 Add vegetables. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes. Toss lightly. To serve, place spaghetti on the serving dish and top with pork and veges.
                  21
                

              
                

                  80
                  Smoky pork and potato traybake - Healthy Food Guide
                  
                  2 tablespoons no-added-salt tomato paste
1 teaspoon smoked paprika
2 teaspoons pure maple syrup
½ teaspoon dried chilli flakes
500g lean pork fillet, cut into 4 even pieces
500g small red potatoes, quartered
16 baby carrots, scrubbed, trimmed, halved if large
1 tablespoon garlic-infused olive oil
250g cherry tomatoes
250g green beans, steamed, to serve
...
                  4
                  Link
                  1 Preheat oven to 190°C. Combine paste, paprika, maple syrup and chilli. Pour half into a large shallow bowl, then add pork, turning to coat. Set aside.
2 Place potatoes in a microwave-safe dish, then cover and microwave on high (100%) for 3 minutes. Drain. Transfer to a large roasting dish. Add carrots, remaining marinade and 2 teaspoons oil, tossing to combine. Bake for 20 minutes.
3 Meanwhile, heat remaining oil in a large, non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat. Add pork and cook, turning halfway, for 2–3 minutes or until browned.
4 Add pork and tomatoes to roasting dish and bake for a further 10–15 minutes or until potatoes are golden and pork is cooked to your liking. Serve traybake with green beans.
                  20
                

              
                

                  73
                  Perfect Pavlova Recipe | Chelsea Sugar
                  
                  6 egg whites (at room temperature)
2 cups Chelsea Caster Sugar (450g)
1 tsp vanilla essence
1 tsp white vinegar
2 tsp Edmonds Fielder's Cornflour
300ml Meadow Fresh Original Cream
whipped
Fruit
to decorate		
                  12
                  Link
                  Preheat oven to 110ºC bake (not fan bake). Line a baking tray with baking paper. In a large metal, ceramic or glass bowl (not plastic), beat the egg whites until soft peaks form. Continue beating while adding the Chelsea Caster Sugar a quarter of a cup at a time. The mixture should get glossier and thicker with each addition and this should take at least 10 minutes. Beat in the vanilla, vinegar and Edmonds Fielder's Cornflour. Spoon mixture out onto the prepared tray into a dinner plate sized mound. Bake for approximately 1 1/2 hours, until dry and crisp and it lifts easily off the baking paper. Turn the oven off and leave the pavlova for at least an hour before removing from the oven. Finish cooling on a wire rack, then transfer to an airtight container. When ready to serve, place on a serving plate, swirl the top with the whipped Meadow Fresh Original Cream and decorate with sliced or chopped fruit of your choice.
                  1
                

              
                

                  14
                  Vanilla Cupcakes with Buttercream Icing Recipe | Chelsea Sugar
                  
                  Cupcakes
150g butter
softened (or Olivani Spread)
1 ½ cups Chelsea Caster Sugar (338g)
2 eggs
2 ½ cups Edmonds Self Raising Flour (375g)
1 ¼ cups Meadow Fresh Milk (310ml)
2 tsp vanilla extract
Buttercream Icing
150g butter
softened (or Olivani Spread)
2 ¼ cups Chelsea Icing Sugar (338g)
2 Tbsp Meadow Fresh Milk
1 ½ tsp vanilla extract
Raspberries
sugar flowers or sprinkles to decorate		
                  24
                  Link
                  In either a heavy - based pan or hob - safe casserole dish, brown the seasoned diced beef with a little oil, then remove from the pan. Do this in two batches. In the same pan, add butter, whole mushrooms, garlic, shallots, thyme and pearl onions. Cook over a medium/high heat, to give them some colour. Add the beef back into the pan and reduce by three quarters. Add in the tarragon, beer and bone broth, bring to a boil, then cover and leave to simmer gently for 2 ½ hours. Stir occasionally and top up with a little water or bone broth if needed. The beef should be just covered with liquid. Preheat the oven to 200 º C. Boil the potatoes in salted water. Once cooked, drain and spread on a lined baking tray. Crush the cooked potatoes with the bottom of a cup, drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Bake in the oven for approximately 25 minutes, until crispy. Once the beef is tender, add in the cream, whiskey and honey. Bring to a boil then season with salt and pepper. Finish with chopped parsley and chives. Sprinkle with fried shallots and serve with crispy crushed potatoes and seasonal vegetables.
                  2
                

              
                

                  11
                  Sweetcorn Fritters Recipe NZ | Edmonds Cooking
                  
                  ​¾ cup Edmonds standard grade flour  1 tsp Edmonds baking powder  ½ tsp salt Black pepper                 1 egg
beaten 440g can cream-style corn 2 Tbsps oil		
                  7
                  Link
                  Sift the flour, baking powder, salt and pepper to taste into a bowl. Add the beaten egg, mixing to combine. Stir in the sweetcorn. Heat the oil in a frying pan. Drop spoonfuls of fritter mixture into the pan. Cook until golden then turn and cook the other side. Drain on absorbent paper. Serve hot.
                  6
                

              
                

                  6
                  Brisket and cheese pies Recipe | Beef + Lamb New Zealand
                  
                  300g Quality Mark beef brisketi
1 onionfinely diced
1 cup tomato passatai
Half a teaspoon mixed herbs
1/4 cup grated tasty cheddar cheesei
A cube beef stock
2 Tbsp balsamic vinegar
2 sheets puff pastry
A dollop Rum & Que 'Meat Juice'
2 garlic cloveschopped
1 cup water
a drizzle Rum & Que 'Slap It On' Mustard		
                  2
                  Link
                  Finely dice the brown onion and garlic. Fry off in a pan with a dash of olive oil and sauté until fragrant and translucent, this should take around 2 minutes. Gently stir through the brisket. Add the beef stock, passata/tomato paste, 'Slap It On' mustard, and mixed herbs to the pan with a cup of water. Cook until the ingredients are combined, the brisket is falling apart, and your desired pie filling consistency is reached. Set your filling aside to cool while you prepare your chosen pie dish or pie maker by lining it with flaky pastry. While the filling is cooling, use pie cutters to cut the base and lid of the pie (the pastry will need to the completely thawed). Turn the pie maker on, once the green light is on you’re good to go! Prepare your pie maker by lining it with the base of the pastry. Fill your pie pastry with a generous scoop of brisket filling, almost to the top, and then top with a generous amount of grated cheese. Add a pastry lid, close the pie maker, and cook until golden brown on top. While the filling is cooling, use pie cutters to cut the base and lid of the pie (the pastry will need to the completely thawed). Prepare your chosen pie dish by lining it with pastry. Fill your pie pastry with a generous scoop of brisket filling, almost to the top, and then top with a generous amount of grated cheese. Add a pastry lid and cook in the oven at 180 °C fan bake until golden brown and hot. This should take between 12-15 minutes. Remove the pie from the pie maker or pie dish and serve with a generous dollop of Rum & Que 'Meat Juice' on top.
                  11
